citations.narrative:
There were two sections of the outdoor penguin enclosure housing 7 African penguins which do not provide sufficient
distance or barriers between the animals and the public to assure the safety of both animals and the public. The 40-inch
tall and 14-inch-thick rock wall on the west side does not prevent a member of the public from reaching over and
potentially making contact with either the penguins or their nest boxes. One of the nest boxes was 12 inches from the
west wall. Additionally, there is a corner section near the bridge walk path over the pool where a member of the public
could potentially reach to make contact with the penguins. In this corner the shortest distance between the 29-inch-tall
barrier out to the rocks and edge of the penguin pool is approximately 12 inches. Without sufficient distance and/or
barriers, the public or the animals could be potentially injured by unauthorized direct contact. Also, the animals may
become harmed or stressed if their nest boxes are disturbed or by items/objects presented to them by people.
During public exhibition, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public, with
sufficient distance and/or barriers between the animal and the general viewing public so as to assure the safety of animals
and the public.
Correct by 03/16/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.131(c)(1) 
   
Handling of animals. 
There were two sections of the outdoor penguin enclosure housing 7 African penguins which do not provide sufficient 
distance or barriers between the animals and the public to assure the safety of both animals and the public. The 40-inch 
tall and 14-inch-thick rock wall on the west side does not prevent a member of the public from reaching over and 
potentially making contact with either the penguins or their nest boxes. One of the nest boxes was 12 inches from the 
west wall. Additionally, there is a corner section near the bridge walk path over the pool where a member of the public 
could potentially reach to make contact with the penguins. In this corner the shortest distance between the 29-inch-tall 
barrier out to the rocks and edge of the penguin pool is approximately 12 inches. Without sufficient distance and/or 
barriers, the public or the animals could be potentially injured by unauthorized direct contact. Also, the animals may 
become harmed or stressed if their nest boxes are disturbed or by items/objects presented to them by people. 
During public exhibition, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public, with 
sufficient distance and/or barriers between the animal and the general viewing public so as to assure the safety of animals 
and the public. 
 
Correct by 03/16/2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
